$schema_note: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Anthology Engine orchestrator (Skill 59). It declares the S0..S9 stage machine, each stage's produces_artifact and gate, the AF-AE-* autofail table with the sibling script and py_symbol that decides each code, the engine-OWNED prompt pins (ae-01..04), the field-map reference, and the COMPLETE shipped-script inventory with purpose and exit-code contract: the 32 normative runtime rows of SPEC 3.4 (rows 1..32) PLUS 4 CI/merge-gate scanner rows (rows 33..36, authored_by W2.8) that ship in scripts/ and so must be inventoried for inventory-equals-shipped-set to hold. A script missing from this inventory does not ship; a shipped script missing from it fails Gate A. schema_version (string "1") matches the SCHEMA_VERSION constant in anthology_state.py so the Gate A build check finds no drift. FINALIZED by the Wave-1 serial integrator from every unit's cross_file_needs: the ae-01..04 sha256 pins are stamped from the W1.18 assets (recomputed and matched); config/route-template.json (W1.6) is in config_refs; caf_delivery.py (row 12, W1.13) exit contract completed to 0/1/2/3/5; the S9 assembly-logic helper stage_s9_assembly_logic.py (W1.18) is inventoried under row 6. The operator delivery-report + signed-process-certificate helper scripts/delivery_report.py (W1.24) ships as the sibling helper imported by caf_delivery.py at S8 and is inventoried under row 12 the same way (no 33rd normative row is added; the four rows 33..36 are CI/merge-gate scanners, not runtime stages).
doctrine: Move in silence (operator-verbose, client-silent; only the three sanctioned nudge templates ever reach a client). Nothing Anthropic ships in any runtime file (guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py + model_router.py deny patterns). Every credential is referenced by label and location only, never by value. The client-facing platform name is Convert and Flow. Config writes run as the node user, never root. Keying is contact_id, never email. Enforcement, not description: every SACRED floor is a fail-closed prover, never advice.

layers.L2_orchestration: The ONLY layer that touches the ledger, the model router, and the gates. intake_router.py -> anthology_state.py -> the stage runners.
layers.L1_authoring_core: Skill 54, invoked as a LOCAL subprocess through 54-anthology-writer/anthology-entry.sh with a working dir per participant per stage; its certification model (hash gate, nonce, fail-closed provers) is preserved untouched.
layers.L3_delivery_adapters: Stateless functions with explicit inputs; every external write is followed by a read-back in the same job (drive_adapter.py, pdf_render.py, caf_delivery.py, cover_render.py).
layers.L4_surfaces: The client's OWN Command Center: the seeded Anthology department board (cards via mc_board.py -> POST /api/tasks/ingest, HMAC + Bearer, fail-soft; review column = approval queue; QC scorer >= 8.5 promotes review to done), the home-screen tiles, and the participant token page. The board holds NO base credential; its only write path is shelling anthology_state.py.

tiers.HEAVY-WRITER: S1 avatar bodies, S2 tone, S3 titles, S4 blurb and outline, S5 chapter, S6 rewrite, S9 introduction and matter (client's strongest NON-Anthropic long-form chain; thinking high, temperature 0.3)
tiers.LIGHT: aw-12 extraction, classification, routing-adjacent chores (Minimax V3 chain)
tiers.JUDGE: Gate B semantic checks and the Tier 2 rubric; ALWAYS a different resolution than the drafting tier (temperature 0)
tiers.LONGCTX: optional S9 whole-manuscript compile (about 1M context) ONLY when the client configured a key; else S9 chunks on HEAVY-WRITER
tiers.IMAGE: S7 cover: the client's own Kie.ai GPT-image-2 PORTRAIT 1024x1536 via the verified text-to-image portrait endpoint (never the 16:9 recipe), through Skills 07 and 46

stages.name: INTAKE AND ROUTING
stages.produces_artifact: participant ledger row (composite key contact_id::anthology_id), Drive path, board card

stages.name: TONE
stages.persona: the Tone Analysts and Blender (tone core 04..08)

stages.name: TITLE
stages.produces_artifact: working/titles.md; Titles Doc plus PDF; TITLE LOCK

stages.name: BLURB AND OUTLINE
stages.produces_artifact: working/blurb.md, working/outline.md; both Docs plus PDFs
stages.persona: the Blurb Copywriter (aw-07) and the Outline Architect (aw-08)

stages.name: CHAPTER
stages.gate: s5_participant (Approve as-is OR Request rewrite with notes)

stages.name: CHAPTER REWRITE
stages.produces_artifact: new chapter VERSION; re-enters the S5 gate

stages.name: COVER IMAGE
stages.produces_artifact: cover PNG in the participant Drive folder; both link fields

stages.name: PACKAGE AND DELIVER
stages.produces_artifact: every deliverable as Google Doc + 14pt-floor PDF; Convert and Flow media + exact-key field writes; control fields; signed certificate
stages.gate: card to review (QC scorer owns review to done)

stages.name: ANTHOLOGY ASSEMBLY
stages.produces_artifact: full manuscript Doc plus PDF; manuscript fields
stages.gate: s9_ready (arm and fire, all PRD 3.11 guards) then s9_producer (sign-off)
stages.persona: the Anthology Editor voice (ae-01..04), subordinate to producer inputs

gate_table.s1_producer: board review column; nudge deep-link in; Approve or Hold
gate_table.s3_selection: participant token page; title and subtitle pick; TITLE LOCK one-way
gate_table.s4_participant: participant token page; outline approval; no re-upload path exists
gate_table.s5_participant: participant token page; exactly two actions; notes feed chapter_updates verbatim
gate_table.s9_ready: the producer ready-to-assemble trigger (Assembly card or readiness nudge, both doors one endpoint); guards: own-producer, every participant approved or excluded, >= min_chapters frozen approved chapters, typed --confirm-name, one-way
gate_table.s9_producer: final manuscript sign-off on the Assembly card; closes the anthology

engine_owned_prompt_pins.$note: The engine OWNS only the S9 assembly and routing prompts. Authored by W1.18; sha256 pins STAMPED by the Wave-1 integrator (each recomputed from the on-disk asset and matched byte-for-byte). Every authoring prompt (aa-01..03, tone 04..08, aw-06..12) lives in Skill 54 / Skill 52 / the tone core and is proven by guard-prompt-pins.py.

script_inventory.script: stage_s0_intake.py, stage_s1_avatar.py, stage_s2_tone.py, stage_s3_title.py, stage_s4_outline.py, stage_s5_chapter.py, stage_s6_rewrite.py, stage_s7_cover.py, stage_s8_deliver.py, stage_s9_assembly.py (ten runners)
script_inventory.authored_by: W1.1 (dispatchers); module logic wired from W1.5..W1.18
script_inventory.purpose: One idempotent thin runner per stage: compose pins, call Layer 1 or the router, run provers, record artifacts, open the gate
script_inventory.exit_codes: 0 stage complete and persisted; 2 prover failure (counts a QC attempt); 3 held (credits or callback, or a collaborator not yet wired); 5 unresolved prompt slot (AF-AE-SLOT-UNRESOLVED)
script_inventory.note: SPEC 3.4 row 6 names nine runners stage_s1..s9; this build also ships stage_s0_intake.py (a thin wrapper over intake_router.py) so S0 has a stage-inventory entry and an exceptions-replay dispatch. SPEC 3.1 names the S4 runner stage_s4_blurb_outline.py; this build ships it as stage_s4_outline.py per the W1.1 file-ownership list. The S9 assembly logic ships as the sibling helper scripts/stage_s9_assembly_logic.py (authored_by W1.18), imported by the stage_s9_assembly.py dispatcher rather than dispatched directly; it is inventoried here under this row so inventory-equals-shipped-set holds without adding a 33rd normative row. W4.0 (integrator): every sibling collaborator has now landed (mc_board.py at W3.1; the W2.1-2.8 guards and provisioning; the fleet roles and SOPs at W4.1-4.9) and all ten dispatcher _invoke_wiring() bodies are wired to concrete collaborator argv chains, in WIRING order, short-circuiting on the FIXED classify_child_rc contract; S9 drives stage_s9_assembly_logic.py's S9Assembly in-process rather than reimplementing its order-curation/compile/sha-verify machinery. Verified structurally (all ten stage_sN.py --self-test pass) and functionally end-to-end against a real temp ledger on the operator box (S0 against this box's live Google Drive credentials; S1/S9 correctly reach and fail-close at the honest boundary requiring live model-chain credentials this build environment does not carry). Full live proof (a real Convert and Flow PIT, a resolved client model-map, Kie.ai image credentials) is the W5 operator-box canary's job, not this unit's.

script_inventory.purpose: FAIL-SOFT Command Center board client (SPEC 11.2): one participant card + one Assembly card per anthology; POST /api/tasks/ingest (create/resolve, idempotent) then POST /api/tasks/{id}/status (move) with Skill 32's existing HMAC (x-webhook-signature over WEBHOOK_SECRET) + Bearer (MC_API_TOKEN) pair; card status mirrors Participants.stage_cursor / anthology assembly_state; producer deliverables land in review (the chapter-approval queue); NEVER emits 'done' (review->done is owned by the QC scorer at >=8.5); board/mirror outage never blocks the pipeline. Reads the local mirror READ-ONLY; anthology_state.py stays the sole writer. Subcommands: sync (create+move), ensure (create only), status (read-only projection), archive (revoke R2: move the Assembly + participant cards to the archived 'blocked' status). Wired into (dispatched by the _invoke_wiring chain of) stage_s0_intake.py, stage_s1_avatar.py, stage_s2_tone.py, stage_s6_rewrite.py, stage_s7_cover.py, stage_s8_deliver.py + stage_s9_assembly.py; stage_s3_title.py / stage_s4_outline.py / stage_s5_chapter.py declare it in their WIRING list but defer the card mirror to the gate-decision path and do not dispatch it directly; archive is invoked by revoke-anthology-client.sh R2.
script_inventory.exit_codes: 0 synced / created / deduped / archived OR fail-soft no-op (board or mirror unavailable, logged and continued); 1 unexpected error; 2 local guard refusal (e.g. an explicit status='done', refused before any I/O)

autofails.trigger: a legacy Make.com/n8n prompt variable slot did not resolve to a canonical field name (SPEC 6.2); the composer refuses any unresolved slot (fail-closed)
a resolved model id or any shipped file carries an Anthropic-family identifier shape
a baked prompt sha256 != its pin, a truncation boundary, or a surviving [UNCHANGED] placeholder
a RENDERED PDF carries a glyph below 14 point
the payload location does not equal the anthology registry Convert and Flow Location binding
missing or invalid hidden ids (contact_id / anthology_id / stage); captured to Exceptions, never dropped or guessed
a stage form arrives for a participant whose cursor does not match; captured to Exceptions (stage_mismatch)
a transition not permitted by the legal-transition matrix (SPEC 7.3); exits nonzero and changes nothing
a Convert and Flow field write or a Drive write does not read back byte-for-byte in the same job
the locked title or subtitle is not carried byte-exact through outline, chapter, every rewrite, and the cover prompt
the chapter measured stripped words fall outside 2,000 to 3,500 (self-report ignored)
a rewrite beyond budget 2; a silent third rewrite is an illegal transition
three internal QC attempts exhausted; HOLD plus one deduped founder alert carrying the failing checks and best draft; standards never relaxed
the JUDGE tier or persona equals the tier or persona that drafted the piece
provider insufficient-credits or chain exhaustion; durable HOLD plus ONE deduped founder Telegram alert through the gateway; resume from the exact cursor
a ready-to-assemble guard failed: non-producer auth, an unapproved and non-excluded participant, below-minimum frozen approved chapters, or a --confirm-name mismatch
a manuscript chapter is not byte-identical (sha256) to its frozen approved artifact
a required PRD Section 6 custom field is absent at provisioning; STOP setup with an operator surface, never a silent runtime create

the Convert and Flow private integration token cannot READ pipelines/opportunities on the location (probed by a read pre-flight -- GoHighLevel exposes no pipeline-create endpoint, so this is never a create-feasibility probe); STOP setup with an operator surface, never a silent fallback

the standard Anthology pipeline is absent on the location and GoHighLevel / Convert and Flow exposes NO public v2 API to create a pipeline (pipelines are UI-only); STOP setup instructing a one-time UI creation or an explicit pre-existing bind (bind --pipeline-id), never a call to a nonexistent create endpoint
any of the nine JSON exports content or a key shape appears in the repo
autofails.enforced_by: scripts/scan-no-json-exports.sh (W2.8 merge-gate export scan; W0.3 originated)

autofails.trigger: more than the one daily tick, or any heartbeat entry; churn leaving a recurring job
an operator, shared, or other-client credential fingerprint is detected in place of the named client's own
a foreign, expired, or replayed participant token
a hand-rolled ungoverned external sender (n8n webhook, Airtable call, literal Authorization key header) is present in a run directory
the enforcement-set content hash does not match the pinned head (ENGINE-PIN.sha256, when present)
a resolved run-dir model-map.json still carries a <CLIENT_*> placeholder (the fleet installer has not resolved this box)
python3 is absent

personas.$note: Mirror of PRD Section 13, stamped into the fleet persona-matching config at W4.2 so the client's workforce self-invokes the right operator per stage. Personas live inside the pinned prompts, never in runtime config.

S2.persona: the Tone Analysts and Blender (tone core 04..08)

S4.persona: the Blurb Copywriter (aw-07) and the Outline Architect (aw-08)

S9.persona: the Anthology Editor voice (ae-01..04), subordinate to producer inputs

content_qc.persona: the independent Editorial Judge on the JUDGE tier

sibling_boundaries.skill_53_book_writer: NOT deprecated, NOT touched: the single-author twelve-chapter product. Executing the stale 'deprecate BookWriter' directive is a scored build failure.
sibling_boundaries.skill_54_anthology_writer: PROMOTED to the authoring core and extended by exactly three bounded extensions (avatar handoff, pinned-prompt completion, intake fields). Building a duplicate anthology skill is a build failure.
sibling_boundaries.tone_core: shared-utils/tone-writing-core reused byte-identical; forking it is a build failure (verify_tone_core_sync.py green).
